description: The US Navy has successfully launched its new Low-cost Uncrewed Combat Attack System (LUCAS) drone from a ship, marking a significant shift in warfare. Developed by reverse-engineering Iranian drones, these affordable 'kamikaze' systems are designed for mass deployment to overwhelm enemy defenses, as seen in Operation Epic Fury against Iran.

description: Suryakumar Yadav celebrates five years in international cricket, a journey marked by a remarkable T20 World Cup triumph.  His impactful debut in 2022 paved the way for a career defined by explosive batting and astute leadership.  Yadav's impressive stats and captaincy record highlight his significant contributions to Team India's success.

title: Did Trump reject Putin's Iran deal to end war? What was the offer
description: Russia's Vladimir Putin reportedly proposed Russia take custody of Iran's enriched uranium to end the ongoing war, a deal Donald Trump declined. This offer comes as the US and Israel seek to secure Iran's nuclear stockpile, with options ranging from voluntary surrender to potential special forces intervention. Trump, however, downplayed the immediate focus on the uranium.

description: The single judge had held that conduct of both the investigation and the departmental inquiry by the officers of the Lokayukta amounts to “inevitable bias” against the employee and offends Article 14 of the Constitution of India

title: PCB mulls legal action after Muzarabani opts for IPL over PSL: Report
description: Pakistan Cricket Board is reportedly considering legal action against Zimbabwe's Blessing Muzarabani. He opted to play for Kolkata Knight Riders in the IPL instead of fulfilling his contract with Islamabad United in the Pakistan Super League. This move, driven by IPL's lucrative opportunities, has angered the PCB, who aim to protect the integrity of their league contracts.

From Delhi's Chandni Chowk to Surat's highways and Karnataka's banks, armed gangs employ deception, intimidation, and violence in carefully planned robberies, highlighting a persistent challenge for law enforcement.

Explosion damages Jewish school in Amsterdam; mayor calls it targeted attack
description: An explosion rocked a Jewish school in Amsterdam, with the mayor suspecting a targeted attack on the community. This follows a similar incident at a Rotterdam synagogue, where arson is suspected. Authorities detained teenagers near another synagogue. An Islamist group has claimed responsibility for both Dutch incidents and a prior attack in Belgium, raising concerns.

Police are investigating domestic tension, possibly linked to the birth of multiple daughters, as a motive. The husband had reportedly been absent since the youngest child's birth.

Gangster groom arrested from marriage venue in Bhopal; fiancée pleads for his release

He and his gang abducted a priest, assaulted him, and forced his brother to transfer Rs 50,000. They threatened to frame the priest in a rape case if he didn't pay an additional Rs 8 lakh.

‘Upset with girlfriend’, Maharashtra man hurls stones at passing train
description: A 23-year-old man in Kalyan allegedly threw stones at a Vande Bharat Express, damaging four coaches. The incident occurred between Thakurli and Kalyan stations after the man reportedly became upset when his girlfriend refused to speak to him on the phone. He admitted to the act, claiming drug consumption. No passengers were injured, but panic ensued.

title: Oil price rise: Market sinks 5% in a week, logs worst 4-yr weekly fall
Women, kids among 40 trapped inside dilapidated building as new owner razes stairs in Kolkata
description: Around 40 residents were trapped in a dilapidated Kolkata building after the staircase was allegedly razed by the new owner. Emergency services used ladders to rescue many, including women and children, from the building slated for real-estate development. The mayor assured tenant protection, while residents expressed concerns about displacement.

title: Irked over charity spend, couple kills mother in Bengal, dumps body in sack
description: A shocking incident unfolded in Howrah where a son and his wife were arrested for allegedly murdering his 62-year-old mother. The victim's body was discovered in a sack. The son confessed to the crime, citing his mother's extensive charitable activities as a financial strain. He claimed his wife was also mistreated by the victim, leading to the brutal act.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet to company’s employees: If you want a promotion, you need to ...
description: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has mandated that employees must demonstrate proficiency in using AI tools to be considered for promotions. This requirement reflects the consultancy's integration of AI into its core operations, a shift Sweet likens to the adoption of computers in the past. The company has invested significantly in reskilling its workforce to adapt to these evolving workplace technologies.

title: Attackers dance after hacking Bengaluru man 68 times with iron straps
description: A fruit vendor, Santosh R, miraculously survived a brutal assault of 68 blows with iron straps on a Bengaluru road. He is an accused in the murder of the attacker Suresh's brother. Suresh and three associates, fueled by revenge, ambushed Santosh after he appeared in court for the murder trial. The attackers fled after the violent incident.

title: Class VI students check Class X papers? Maharashtra Board to re-evaluate
description: The Kolhapur division of the Maharashtra Board has requested an inquiry into a viral video allegedly showing Std VI students evaluating Std X Marathi exam papers. A preliminary report suggests the children were marking blank sections to prevent cheating, not grading. Officials will visit the school to verify claims and teacher authorization.

title: Cognizant advises BYOF for employees, reviews supply concerns
description: Cognizant is encouraging employees to bring their own food as part of contingency planning amid the West Asia crisis. The IT major is preparing for potential disruptions in food services and transport due to energy market volatility. The company is exploring alternative vendors and energy sources to mitigate risks.

title: Village throws party for ‘IAS officer’, later discover UPSC rank belongs to someone else
description: What began as a joyous occasion in a Bihar village quickly spiraled into a tale of disappointment when Ranjeet Kumar's announcement of conquering the UPSC civil services exam was revealed to be an elaborate ruse.

title: UP woman ‘revived’ by jolt on NH-74: NHAI says ‘no potholes’ on stretch but ground reality different
description: A woman critically ill and believed to be brain dead revived after the ambulance carrying her home hit a pothole-ridden stretch of National Highway-74. Despite NHAI claims of a pothole-free road, a TOI team found damaged patches, including cracks and depressions, on the route.
